steps.title: Understand vertical shifts
steps.content: Shifting a graph DOWN by $k$ units subtracts $k$ from the output: $g(x) = f(x) - k$.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $g \left(x\right)=\frac{5}{4}{x}^{3}$: This scales the function vertically by $\frac{1}{4}$, not a vertical shift. Incorrect
  • Choice B — $g \left(x\right)=5{x}^{\frac{3}{4}}$: This changes the exponent, producing a completely different function. Incorrect
  • Choice C — $g \left(x\right)=5{x}^{3}+4$: Adding 4 shifts the graph UP 4 units, not down. Incorrect
  • Choice D — $g \left(x\right)=5{x}^{3}-4$: Subtracting 4 shifts the graph DOWN 4 units. Correct
That confirms D is correct.
